Afghanistan is a country located in Central Asia.
Kent Brockman during a news cast reported Kamp Krusty surpassed Afghanistan, Vietnam and Iraq.
Albania is a former Communist country located in eastern Europe and the home of Adil Hoxha.

After the conclusion of World War II, Albania was taken over by Communism and was part of the Eastern Bloc in Europe. In the late 1980s, the Communist government was attempting to learn the secrets of the USA's nuclear technology. To that end, they sent a child spy named Adil Hoxha to Springfield to learn all he could about Springfield Nuclear Power Plant and send the information back to the Albanian government.

Adil went to the United States as part of the foreign exchange student program, arriving in Springfield on a regular commercial flight. His mission was a success, as Adil obtained a great deal of information (including blueprints) about the nuclear plant from an unwitting Homer Simpson. Adil also persuaded Homer to give him a tour of the plant, and during the tour Adil took many photos of the plant's equipment. He then sent all of the information back to his superiors in Albania. However, the radio and fax transmissions attracted the attention of the FBI, and Adil was caught when they traced the source to the Simpson home. Rather than being punished, Adil was deported from the United States and exchanged for an American child spy who had been caught in Albania.
After the fall of Communism, Albania competed in the 2010 Winter Olympics in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

Ironically, the fall of Communism in Albania occurred in December 1990, eight months after Adil Hoxha's appearance on The Simpsons. Unlike most Communist nations in Europe, Communist Albania had a better advantage for political survival due to the decision which the country's Communist government made to end its alliance with the Soviet Union in the 1960s and instead join the Non-Aligned Movement.

Algeria (French: Algérie, Arabic: الجزائر), officially the People's Democratic Republic of Algeria (Arabic: الجمهورية الجزائرية الديمقراطية الشعبية, French: République algérienne démocratique et populaire), is a country in North Africa on the Mediterranean coast. Its capital and most populous city is Algiers. With an area of 2,381,741 square kilometres (919,595 sq mi), Algeria is the tenth-largest country in the world, and the largest in Africa and the Arab world. Algeria is bordered to the northeast by Tunisia, to the east by Libya, to the west by Morocco, to the southwest by Western Sahara, Mauritania, and Mali, to the southeast by Niger, and to the north by the Mediterranean Sea. The country is a semi-presidential republic consisting of 48 provinces and 1,541 communes. Abdelaziz Bouteflika, who fought in the war against the French colony, has been President since 1999.

Ancient Algeria has known many empires and dynasties, including ancient Numidians, Phoenicians, Carthaginians, Romans, Vandals, Byzantines, Umayyads, Abbasids, Idrisid, Aghlabid, Rustamid, Fatimids, Zirid, Hammadids, Almoravids, Almohads, Ottomans and the French colonial empire. Berbers are generally considered to be the indigenous inhabitants of Algeria. Following the Arab conquest of North Africa, most indigenous inhabitants were Arabised; thus, although most Algerians are Berber in origin, most identify with Arabic culture. En masse, Algerians are a mix of Berbers, Arabs, Turks and Black Africans.

The north African country supplies large amounts of natural gas to Europe, and energy exports are the backbone of the economy. According to OPEC Algeria has the 17th largest reserves of oil in the world, and the second largest in Africa, while it has the 9th largest reserves of natural gas. Sonatrach, the national oil company, is the largest company in Africa. Algeria has one of the largest military in Africa and the largest defence budget in the whole continent, they import most of their weapons from Russia whom is a close ally. Algeria is a member of the African Union, the Arab League, OPEC, the United Nations and is the founding member of the Maghreb Union.

Andorra, officially known as the Principality of Andorra, is a micronation located between France and Spain within the Pyrenees Mountains in Europe. The official language is Catalan, yet people speak Spanish.
Andorra's observers were seen at the Vancouver Olympic Games in 2010.
Angola is a country located in southern Africa.
Angolan Olympic Team are part of the opening ceremony for the Vancouver Olympic Games.

Argentina, officially the Argentine Republic (Spanish: República Argentina), is the second largest country in Latin America by land area, the fourth largest country in the Americas after USA, Canada and Brazil, the eighth-largest country in the world by land area and the largest among Spanish-speaking nations. It is one of the southernmost countries in South America.

Argentina was mentioned several times on The Simpsons, first in Treehouse of Horror II when Lisa wishes for world peace to the magic monkey hand. When it is declared, the representatives of England and Argentina at the United Nations embrace each other.

Bart ordering pizza at home delivered by Krusty, but when the clown arrives, Bart turns on the television and see that the show is not live. In the program being broadcast, Krusty gets last-minute cable and says, "Uh! Children, stay calm. The Falkland Islands have just been invaded. I repeat, the Falklands have just been invaded! The plot unfolds when a map is opened and explained, "The Falklands are here -with a pointer- near the coast of Argentina."

Lisa tells her brother that the water runs in a different sense in the northern hemisphere than in the South. As Bart does not understand, Lisa tells him to look over the globe. Everything below the line of Ecuador is the southern hemisphere and hence the water turning in another sense, he says. "You mean that the Argentina -points on the globe- the water runs backwards?" Asked Bart.

Lisa writes the International Olympic Committee and they decide to make the Olympics in Springfield. In search of the headquarters of the female representative of Argentina in Buenos Aires proposed Committee.
Argentina Representative of the committee.

When Selma throws a bucket of fire ants (Solenopsis invicta) to the sink. These ants are not native to the United States, and recently found that arrived there in the decade 1930-40 as stowaways on ships that came from Argentina.
During the presentation of the 2010 Winter Olympics in Vancouver, the team of Argentina is the third go to the games.
Armenia is a country located in Eurasia, that was once part of the Soviet Union.
Armenia was briefly mentioned by Moe Szyslak when he remarked that "Armenia Idol" was his favorite television show.
Moe Szyslak: Mentioned that he's "Half monster-half Armenian" and watches Armenia idol.
Seymour Skinner: His real name is Armin Tamzarian which is an Armenian name.
Dr. Egoyan: Armenian last name and based off of real life Armenian Doctor Jack Kevorkian.
Aruba is an island which is part the kingdom of the Netherlands which is located in North America.

Australia is a country in the Pacific Ocean, part of Oceania. It has a total area of 7,688,287 km2 (2,968,464 sq mi), making it the sixth-largest country in the world and the largest in Oceania. Australia is the world's flattest and driest inhabited continent.

The Simpsons visit Australia when Bart makes a prank call asking a boy what direction the water goes when he flushes the toilet. Bart then leaves the phone off the hook, raking up a huge phone bill for them as he made a reverse call. The guy sues him and Bart has to travel to a court in Australia. He unwittingly sets his bullfrog Bart Junior free, and this results in an invasive species devastating the Australian ecosphere. This is a reference to cane toads, which are a poisonous species of toad introduced to Australia, which are now a pest found in New South Wales, Queensland and the Northern Territory, considered a pest.
Database represented Australia in the Model U.N. Club.

Bart ended up calling an Australian bar using Denis Leary's stolen cell phone to play a prank call. Specifically, he called a pub called Crocodile Drunkee's requesting for a "Drew P. Wiener" causing the barman to call out for the person and stating, "I have in my hand a ‘Drew P. Wiener’." The patrons laugh at him and tell him that he'd "better put it down, mate!", to the barman's frustration.
According to Abe, the Simpson forbears were once kicked out of Australia.
New Zealand is mentioned to be the Milhouse of Australia.

In "Bart vs. Australia", they used corporal punishment, the most popular method of which is "the boot", which is so popular it features on the national flag. This is of course, fictitious, and neither the flag nor the punishment appear in real-life.
Australia has been mentioned a total of 68 times in The Simpsons.
Rainier Wolfcastle was originally from Austria, and is seen featured in an Austrian advertisement. This is a direct parody of Arnold Schwarzenegger as he was also born in Austria.

Adolf Hitler, the leader of the Nazi Party was born in Braunau am Inn, Austria-Hungary on 20 April 1889. He would later become Chancellor, then Führer of Germany and rule Germany as a dictatorship from 1933-45. Hitler was responsible for starting World War II when German forces invaded Poland on 1 September 1939. Austria was annexed by Nazi Germany in 1938, as well as the murders and displacements of thousands of Jews living there.
Azerbaijan (Azerbaijani: Azərbaycan), officially the Republic of Azerbaijan, is a country located in Eurasia, that was part of the Soviet Union.
Lisa would have represented Azerbaijan in the Model U.N. Club, though she abandoned it.
Azerbaijan is one of the many countries seen represented at the Vancouver Olympics.
The Bahamas is a tropical country located about 50 miles (80 km) south-east of Florida.
The flag of the Bahamas is hanging behind Homer when he hides in a bobsled while at the 2010 Winter Olympics held in Vancouver.
Bart has temporary hearing lost and Dr. Hibbert tells Homer, "The boy's hearing should clear up in a day. If it doesn't, call me in the Bahamas."
Bangladesh (Bengali: বাংলাদেশ), officially the People's Republic of Bangladesh (Bengali: গণপ্রজাতন্ত্রী বাংলাদেশ), is a country in southern Asia.
While at the Simpson's house, after having dinner, Krusty finds a record with music from Bangladesh. As he plays it, however, the family kick him out of the house since he had overstayed his welcome.
Apu Nahasapeemapetilon owns a record called Concert Against Bangladesh.
Belarus is a country located in eastern Europe, and was once part of the Soviet Union.
Belarus was represented at the Model U.N. Club.
Belarus was one of the many nations represented at the Vancouver Olympics.

Belgium is a country located in Europe. It is the neighboring country of the Netherlands, Germany, Luxembourg and France. The country consists of Flanders, where they speak Dutch and Wallonia, where they speak French and German.
Martin Prince represented Belgium in the Model U.N. Club.
Belgium is one of the many nations represented at the 2010 Winter Olympics.
It is revealed that Bumblebee Man is from Belgium.
Superintendent Chalmers became interested in craft brewing at his home and had a poster hanging in the garage captioned BELGIUM showing Orval Abbey, a monastery and producer of Trappist beer.
Belize is a country located in southern North America; its capital city is Belmopan.
Belize is one of the many nations represented at the 2010 Winter Olympics.

One of Patty and Selma's vacations was to Bolivia and brought back a souvenir Bolivian coin collection for Bart. Bart, looking for money to buy Issue 1 of Radioactive Man comics, takes the coins to the Foreign Exchange at the local bank branch to "Americanize this," but then disappointedly learns "All those coins were only worth three lousy cents?" and the man of the Foreign Exchange quips back, "Let the good times roll!"

Marge and Homer are at the ticket window for the Springfield Googolplex Theatres and decide to see "The Stockholm Affair" which Marge read in the newspaper was suppose to be a "taunt political thriller," but just the mention of political causes Homer to groan. During the movie a general in the Oval Office reports, "Mr. President, disturbing news. Serious cracks are developing in the Greco–Bolivian alliance."
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Bolivia is one of the countries seen.
Chirpy Boy and Bart Junior are Bolivian Tree Lizards.
Chalmers and Skinner attempt to flee to Bolivia only for Bart to snag Skinner's car keys.
Episode – "Three Men and a Comic Book" ("Coins of Bolivia" souvenir)
Episode – "The Old Man and the "C" Student" (Titicaca mentioned, a lake in both Bolivia and Peru)
Episode – "You Kent Always Say What You Want" (Titicaca mentioned, a lake in both Bolivia and Peru)
Bosnia and Herzegovina is a country located in South-Eastern Europe; it was formerly part of Yugoslavia before gaining independence in 1992.
Botswana is a country located in Africa.
Big Ass Baryshnikov, Homer's video, becomes viral. Botswanan is the nationality of one of the many people viewing the video.

Brazil (Portuguese: Brasil), officially the Federative Republic of Brazil (Portuguese: República Federativa do Brasil), is the largest and the only Portuguese-speaking country in South America. It is the world's fifth largest country both by geographical area and population.
At the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant, a female agent of the Department of Labor mentions that they found a missing Brazilian soccer team working in the reactor core.
Marge said that a baby could not of organized a big school dance. Unless it was especially skilled or one of those super-babies from Brazil.
The Simpsons (excluding Maggie) visit Brazil where they try to find an orphan named Ronaldo and Homer gets kidnapped. It has rats that look like Skittles.
Homer had also said that he wouldn't mind going back to Brazil but he had heard that the monkey problem had got a lot worse.

When the Simpsons go to Barnacle Bay, which was Marge's old childhood paradise is now a disgusting dump. To which Lisa says that this is worse place that the family has ever gone too. Bart then replies what about Brazil, to which Lisa replies after Brazil.

Ronaldo, a Brazilian soccer player, once comes to Springfield to point out that Lisa is flopping all the day while playing soccer what made Homer as a referee to give her a yellow card and then a red card. Ronaldo also teaches the Simpson family how to play soccer.
Brazil was one of the team competing in the 2010 Winter Olympics.
The Simpsons come to Brazil for Homer to coach Soccer.
The Simpsons aired on Rede Globo, SBT and TV Bandeirantes on Brazil.
Episode – "Father Knows Worst" (Brazilian National Congress model)
Bulgaria is a country located in Europe.
Spinal Tap mention that they are very big in Bulgaria and "Hungaria."
Bulgaria can be seen on the map of World War One in Edna Krabappel's fourth grade classroom.
Burkina Faso is a country located in Africa.

Bart calls this nation while trying to contact the southern hemisphere, despite it existing north of the equator. When Homer sees the phone bill three weeks later, he is confused as to who would call Burkina Faso.
Cambodia (Khmer: កម្ពុជា។), officially the Kingdom of Cambodia (Khmer: ព្រះរាជាណាចក្រកម្ពុជា។), is a country located in Southeast Asia.
Cambodia is one of the many countries seen on Bart's globe.
Cameroon is a country located in Africa.

Canada is the second largest country in the world (in terms of land mass) and located north of the continental United States of America. The Simpsons have gone to Canada and specifically Toronto in "The Bart Wants What it Wants" and Calgary in "I'm Spelling as Fast as I Can" and Vancouver in "Boy Meets Curl" and Niagara Falls (across the Niagara River from Upstate New York) and Windsor (across the Detroit River from Detroit) in "D'oh Canada".

Homer referred to Canada as "America Junior" and another time when apologizing to Queen Elizabeth II for wrecking her carriage, he also disdainfully said of Canada, "But we Americans are England's children. I know we don't call as often as we should, and we aren't as well-behaved our goody-two-shoes brother, Canada-- who, by the way, has never had a girlfriend. I'm just saying..." He also called Canada, "the Mexico of Alaska."

A recurring joke is Homer being bored by things happening in Canada such as skipping the barbeque at the Flanderses' to watch CFL Draft or trying to get out of an awkward conversation with Marge by reading "Canada to Hold Referendum" in the newspaper.

Another recurring gag is similarities between Canada and Ned Flanders.. In Canada Ned meets a nearly identical Canadian stranger with the same overly good-natured gibberish way of speaking. Kent Brockman refers to Canada as "America's Ned Flanders to the North" during his news report.
Bart, Milhouse, and Martin tell their parents that they going by their school to attend the National Grammar Rodeo in Canada as a cover story.
Ralph Wiggum represented Canada in the Model U.N. Club.

Greta Wolfcastle breaks off her relationship with Bart and heads to Canada because her dad Rainier Wolfcastle is shooting his latest movie in Toronto so Bart follows her to try to win back and the rest of the Simpsons come with him to sightsee in Toronto.

The Canadian national anthem was also sung at the end of a baseball game after a fight broke out because of the Pre-Teen Braves and the Cavalry Kids. Homer, Grampa, Apu and Ned all visited Canada to get prescription drugs from there into Springfield but they got arrested at the border when it was discovered their van was full of pills (Homer and Grampa were arrested again, namely because they crashed their plane and woke Chief Wiggum up).
Bart tells Lisa his mnemonic "Ouiet Nerds Burp Only Near School." for the 4 original provinces when Canada became self-governing on July 1, 1867: Quebec, New Brunswick, Ontario, Nova Scotia.

Patty and Selma have kidnapped Richard Dean Anderson, star of their favorite TV show MacGyver, but then he will not leave. He is finally broken by slides of different carriages from their vacation in Canada to the Remington Carriage Museum.
Lisa pretends to be from Canada as Europe is under the impression that United States has made some stupid choices in the past, however, her cover is blown by Homer when waves the American flag.
Mr. Burns wants Smithers to get him a quarter ton of lake ice from Northern Canada and Smithers has harrowing misadventures bringing it back.

Marge, Bart, Lisa and Maggie are sightseeing from the windows of the crown on the Statue of Liberty and Bart yells out, "Hey, immigrants! Beat it! Country's full!" to a group of anachronistic turn-of-the-20th century foreigners on the deck of a steamer. The captain tells them, "Okay, folks, you heard the lady. Back into the hold. We'll try Canada" which is greeted by disappointed moans.

In 1860 fugitive slave Virgil crosses the border into freedom in Canada. Before fleeing he asked Mabel Simpson to come with him which Mabel accepted and they stay in Canada for the rest of their lives. Mabel marries Virgil after she divorced Hiram Simpson from Canada and getting just one of his shoes in the divorce settlement although Hiram kept the laces.

When the Simpsons go to Niagara Falls, Lisa goes over the waterfalls. She is rescued by a Mountie and given political asylum in Canada while recovering in the hospital. When Marge goes to Canada to rescue Lisa, they begin to re-evaluate their love for America. Later she bring the same Mountie to show-and-tell. The Mountie has brought back Nelson's dad who was hiding out in Medicine Hat.
Chile, officially the Republic of Chile (Spanish: República de Chile), is a country of South America.

When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Chile is one of the countries seen upon the globe. After Bart makes several calls to numerous countries Homer receive a phone bill, one of the place on the bill include Santiago, Chile.
It was from a Krusty store in China that the 'panda virus' came to Springfield, as a mosquito got into the Bart's laffy meal's toy dinosaur.
The Simpson family visited China along with Selma to get her an adopted child, Ling. Homer had to pretend to be Selma's husband, as China only allows married couples to adopt.
China served in the Vietnam War, which is sometimes mentioned in the series.

Radioactive Man was going to go to China and get some takeout when he hears a Sky Tweet from Commissioner Sweeney. He then discovers the Fossil Fuel Four at a Nuclear Plant, who killed him in 4 different stages
China, or rather, the pre-Communist version, was the implied location of the Itchy & Scratchy episode "The Great Brawl Of China".
In "Goo Goo Gai Pan", Taiwan on the map of China in Consulate of China's color is different from China's color, it's green. This is probably talking about the relationship between Taiwan and China.
In "Mr. Lisa's Opus", Lisa holds a sign saying “FREE CHINA FROM TIBET”, suggesting that Tibet may have invaded China.

Costa Rica is a country in Central America.
Krusty accuses a man of continuing his stereotype as a Mexican. The man tells him he is from Costa Rica, but Krusty doesn't listen.
Megan Matheson is bidding on Joan Miró's The Poetess for her private monkey sanctuary in Costa Rica because "the monkeys find the colors quite arousing."

The Van Houtens take an annual vacation to a luxury villa in Costa Rica and select other families to join them and one year the Simpsons finally get an invite. The Simpsons figured out Van Houtens are scamming everyone else by making them pay rent while hiding the Van Houtens stay for free because the luxury villa was first built by their distant relative Kirkedemious Van Houten and still owned by the family.
Croatia is a country located in Europe. It has a curling team.
The Pink Sedan was made in Croatia from old Soviet tanks.
Cuba is an island country located in the Caribbean, about ninety miles south of the Florida coast.

Homer, Mr. Burns and Smithers visit Cuba when they are on the run from the U.S. Federal Government. Mr. Burns tries to buy Cuba with a trillion-dollar bill, but Fidel Castro steals the bill and the trio are forced out of the country.
Episode – "Special Edna" (mentioned, Cuban Missile Crisis)
Cyprus (Greek: Κύπρος; Turkish: Kıbrıs), officially the Republic of Cyprus, is an island country of Eurasia.
Czechia, officially the Czech Republic, is a country in the middle Europe.
The Male Golem comes from Prague, the capital city of Czechia. Prague Oldnew Synagogue can je seen on screen.
The former country Czechoslovakia, of which Czechia was a part of, has also been mentioned before in The Simpsons by Patty and Selma.
Democratic Republic of the Congo (known as Zaire from 1971 to 1997) is a country of Africa.
Denmark (Danish: Danmark) is a country located in Europe.
Kearney played Earthland Realms while he was in Denmark, somehow with no internet issues.
Norbert Van Houten is a Dane (from Denmark).

The TV commercial for the Danish weight-loss drug Othinquic has an anthropomorphic Othinquic pen (voiced by Danish actress Sidse Babett Knudsen) flying out of a drug store in Denmark. The real Ozempic which Othinquic is a thinly veiled parody of is made in Kalundborg, Denmark by Danish pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk. ("The Last Man Expanding")
Episode – "I Am Furious (Yellow)" (Niels Bohr is from Denmark)
Episode – "Game Done Changed" (Denmark currency)

Djibouti gained independence from France in 1977. A runner from Djibouti competed in Springfield Marathon. With 500 yards to go, he was neck-and-neck with the competitor from Australia. However, they were both beaten by Bart who cheated win and was masked as an Italian.
Dominican Republic is a country located on the island Hispaniola in the Greater Antilles archipelago of the Caribbean region.
Egypt, officially the Arab Republic of Egypt, is a country located in Africa. A version of the show that changed Western references was produced and broadcast on MBC.
Patty and Selma trip to Egypt is shown on their slideshow.
In 1200 B.C., the Jews have fled Egypt and decide to set up camp at Mount Sinai.
Homer and Lisa break into the Springsonian Museum to see the Egyptian exhibit.

Lisa (as Miriam) and Milhouse (as Moses) command Skinner (as King Ramses II) to let their people go. After several failed plagues and an escape from a pyramid prison, they lead the Israelites to the shore, simultaneously flush all the Egyptians' toilets to drain the sea. After they have crossed the seabed, the water fills the sea back up, washing away the pursuing Pharaoh and his guards. Moses asks Lisa if they will have an easy life from now on - she tells him that they will wander the desert for forty years. When he asks if it's plain sailing after that, she quickly changes the subject.
Comic story – The Mighty Moe Szyslak (Ancient Egypt, 1500 B.C.)
One of the actors Charles Montgomery Burns hires to swindle Bart is an Estonian Dwarf.
Martin Prince represented Finland's culture for their recreation of the U.N at the Model U.N. Club.
Finland is seen being represented at the International Olympic Committee.

Agnes Skinner represented the United States as a pole vaulter in the 1952 Summer Olympics held in the capital of Finland, Helsinki, where she lost thanks to her being heavily pregnant with Seymour Skinner.

A-Team of Finland appeared after the Chinese Intelligence and French Bureau of Investigation to get Bart for nuclear information. The team consists of a man named B. A. Bäckström and an another unnamed spy.
France (Breton: Bro-C'hall), officially the French Republic (French: République française), is a country in western Europe.

Bart, as part of scheme from Principal Skinner to get rid of him, participates in student exchange program to spend 3 months in France. he is being exploited through force manual labor by winemakers and criminals César and Ugolin.
Lisa represented France in the Model U.N. Club.
Whole Simpson family escaped to France after Bart mistakenly mooned the American flag.
Krusty came to France to find Princess Penelope after he left her.
The Bouvier family are of French origin, and is also implied that Jacques is from France.
There is a little town in France with a statue of Grampa Simpson surrendering during World War II.
In The Simpsons Game, France is also a place in the eating contest Around the World in 80 Bites. In the level "Medal of Homer", Homer and Bart had to save France by removing all the surrender flags.

In the "Treehouse of Horror VIII" segment "The HΩmega Man," Mayor Quimby insults France while telling a "frogs leg joke." France demanded that Mayor Quimby apologize or suffer the consequences. Quimby unwisely refused to apologize and told them to "do their worst." In retaliation the French launch a nuclear attack on Springfield, killing almost everyone except the Simpsons, while mutating several other surviving citizens.
In the story of Hot Child in the City France and England are in a century war.
In the story Henry VIII, Margerine of Aragon mentions that she could of married the king of France.
The Count of Monte Fatso story takes place during 19th Century France where Moe Szyslak breaks up Homer and Marge by framing Homer as a English traitor.
Episode – "Das Bus" (Lisa represents France in the Model U.N. Club)
French Polynesia are a French territory in the Pacific Ocean comprised of a series of islands. It's an overseas country of France.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, French Polynesia is one of the countries seen upon the globe.
The Bounty crew has a vacation in Tahiti one of the French Polynesian islands.
Saakashvili Spring (one of the nominated films in "Angry Dad: The Movie") takes place in the Republic of Georgia.
Georgian clothing designer Demna creates a "Balenciaga Salutes Springfield" fashion show in Paris.
Georgia is the country of origin of The Samsonadzes, a computer-animated series considered by many as a rip-off of The Simpsons.

Germany (German: Deutschland), officially the Federal Republic of Germany (German: Bundesrepublik Deutschland), is the most-populated country in Europe. Two German businessmen, Hans and Fritz took over the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant and brought in German workers like Horst. Üter Zörker originates from Düsseldorf, Germany and Coach Lugash escaped East Germany by cartwheeling over the Berlin Wall. It was known for its production of various chocolates, which caused it to be nicknamed the Land of Chocolate as Hans and Fritz mentioned when taking over the plant. However, Homer took their statement to mean something else. The town of Humbleton in Pennsylvania has an architecture similar to Germanic countries.
Between 1933 and 1945, Germany was a totalitarian dictatorship controlled by Adolf Hitler and the Nazi Party. It annexed multiple parts of Europe, including neighboring country Austria.

Between 1949 and 1990, Germany was split into two separate countries; the Federal Republic of Germany (West Germany) and the German Democratic Republic (East Germany). Berlin was also infamously divided by the Berlin Wall between 1961 and 1989. The two divided nations reunified in a formal ceremony on 3 October 1990.

As a federal state Germany consists of 16 federated states. Each state is governed by a head of government (going by a different title in the city states of Berlin, Hamburg and Bremen), whose position is roughly the same as that of the Governor of a US state.
Angela Merkel was the Chancellor of Germany from 2005 to 2021. Olaf Scholz is the current chancellor.
In 1945, Mr. Burns' teddy bear Bobo floats into Germany where it is found by Adolf Hitler.
When Springfield nearly win the chance to host the Olympic Games, Bart insults Germany by saying "How ya doin', Germany? Here's my impression of an East German woman. Kiss me or I'll crush you".
Brunella Pommelhorst states that claims that Lugash defected into Germany, by "cartwheeling over the Berlin Wall".
Germany was also one of the levels in Around the World in 80 Bites, the third level in The Simpsons Game.
One of Bart's prank calls to destroy Lisa's baby sitting credibility is a call to a driver to pick the Ghana Ambassador.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Ghana is one of the countries seen upon the globe.
Ms. Peyton, the new 4th grade teacher at Springfield Elementary School, previously taught in Ghana for 2 years.
Homer goes to a baseball with the new employee at Springfield Nuclear Power Plant who is originally from Ghana and he does not understand baseball.
Greece is a Mediterranean country in Europe.
"The Answer was discovered by the philosophers of Ancient Greece." according to the advertising.
"Art We Stole From Greece" façade banner at Springfield Metropolitan Museum.
Fox News' ticker: Europe puts Greece on eBay
Homer reads Homer's The Odyssey to Bart, Lisa and Bart which takes place on Ithaca and neighbor islands all in present-day Greece.
Among Krusty's many charges by the International Court in The Hague was "...in Greece you committed something called aggravated Hey-Hey."

Spiro’s, a biker bar in Springfield, is owned by Greek immigrant Spiro Papadapaconstantikasgianopolopodopotopolis who laments, "Why did I ever leave Greece? Oh, that's right, 'cause it's a train wreck."
Dr. Marvin Monroe appears on Smartline with the chyron "Live from Athens, Greece" where he is in front of the Parthenon on top of the Acropolis.
Springfield Metropolitan Museum's façade banner: "Art We Stole From Greece."
Text moves across the bottom of the screen on Fox News when Homer is a panelist on Head Butt with Nash Castor: "Europe puts Greece on eBay."

Sideshow Bob, having framed Krusty for armed robbery of the Kwik-E-Mart and then taken over The Krusty the Clown Show, is in the middle of saying, "In Ancient Greece, there was a school of thought called stoicism." when Bart has pieced together the clue and yells out live on TV that Sideshow Bob framed Krusty.

Greenland (Greenlandic: Kalaallit Nunaat, Danish Grønland) is an autonomous country in North America, owned by Denmark. It is the world's largest island (Australia and Antarctica are larger, but Antarctica is a continent and Australia is sometimes called a continental landmass). Over 85% of the population is Inuit and just over 7% is European.
Homer ate chili made by Clancy Wiggum at the Springfield chili cook-off. The chili contained Guatemalan Insanity Pepper.
Fox Mulder mentions voodoo priests of Haiti.
There is a veteran from Haiti on "Veterans of Unpopular Wars" hall.
A restaurant at the Springfield Squidport is named "What They Eat In Iceland."
Carl once said that he spent part of his boyhood in Iceland.
A team from Iceland is seen competing in the 2010 Winter Olympics.

After Carl cheats Homer, Lenny and Moe by running off with their lottery money, the three discover, after looking at a picture of Carl, that he is from Iceland. Further enquiries reveal that Carl was adopted by the Carlson family, that is hated in Iceland due to their failure to hold back the Viking invasion centuries ago.

In Simpsons Comics #162: "Oh, Brother!" Homer Mr. Burns start acting like brothers when they learn Abe has suddenly married Mr. Burns' mother. In the end Abe had actually married Dame Tallulah Winkelman, an acclaimed actress Smithers hired to pretend to be Mrs. Burns. The final gag at the end of the story is Homer making a phone call because his banker extended his line of credit after Homer told him he was Mr. Burns' brother and Homer now has to call off his purchase of Iceland which he renamed Homerland.

India (Hindi and Marathi: भारत; Urdu: ہندوستان; Bengali: ভারত; Punjabi: ਭਾਰਤ; Gujarati: ભારત; Kannada: ಭಾರತ; Tamil: இந்தியா; Telugu: భారతదేశం Bhāratadēśaṁ; Malayalam: ഇന്ത്യ), officially the Republic of India (Hindi: भारत गणराज्य; Urdu: جمہوریہ ہندوستان; Bengali: ভারতীয় প্রজাতন্ত্র; Punjabi: ਭਾਰਤ ਗਣਰਾਜ; Gujarati: ભારતીય ગણતંત્ર; Kannada: ಭಾರತ ಗಣರಾಜ್ಯ; Tamil: இந்தியக் குடியரசு; Telugu: భారత గణతంత్ర రాజ్యము; Malayalam: ഭാരതമഹാരാജ്യം), is a country in South Asia and is the second-most populous country in the world, with a population of 1,324,000,000, and is where Kwik-E-Mart owner Apu was born.

The country's borders with: Pakistan, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, China and Myanmar. The Springfield Nuclear Power Plant was temporarily outsourced to India and Homer becomes a supervisor for the Plant. There, he meets a relative of Apu.

There are more than 23 recognised regional languages in India, and the country became independent from the United Kingdom as a doninion on 15 August 1947, before declaring itself a republic on 26 January 1950. The largest city in India is Mumbai (formerly known as Bombay) with a population of 12,400,000. The current Prime Minister is Narendra Modi, who is head of government and has served since 26 May 2014. The current President, is Ram Nath Kovind, who is head of state, and has served since 25 July 2017.
Krusty the Clown remarked that India nuked Pakistan.
To rescue a cow Bart raised, Apu shipped the cow by air to India since cows are sacred animals in the country.
Indonesia, officially the Republic of Indonesia (Indonesian: Republik Indonesia), is a country of Asia.

Mr. Burns is trying to charm Homer and Marge at Burns Manor so they will not suing him for hitting Bart with his car. The room is filled with stuffed animals mounted on the walls and Mr. Burns told them, "And that ugly customer was the last Indonesian Rhino on Earth." Marge did not know he liked animals which Mr. Burns quips, "I don't like everything about them. Just their heads." and he and Smithers chuckle.
Homer is seen reading The Economist, "Indonesia's Gambit." He is seen later reading The Economist again, this time titled "New challenges for Indonesia."
Indonesia is seen on Bart's global of the world, but inaccurately shows Borneo and Sulawesi to be connected, and the location of Singapore.

Reverend Kartawijaya from Indonesia is the guest speaker during Sunday service at First Church of Springfield telling the story about a little boy in his village named Sumadi. Bart envisioned Sumadi's pranks backfiring in the Indonesian jungle.
Iran is a country in Western Asia. It is bordered by Turkey, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Iraq, Turkmenistan, Afghanistan, and Pakistan. Its capital is Tehran.
Iraq is a country in the Middle East. It is bordered by Iran, Turkey, Syria, Jordan (country), Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait.
Kent Brockman claims Kamp Krusty is worse than Vietnam, Afghanistan and Iraq.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Iraq is one of the countries seen upon the globe.
In "Tennis the Menace", Kent Brockman mishears a joke and retells it by saying "I guess you can say I'm Iraqi".
In "Milhouse of Sand and Fog", after Homer is fired from his job when tell his boss that he has chicken pox Homer see a newspaper advertisement which reads "TRUCKERS NEEDED IN IRAQ".
Episode – "Tennis the Menace" (Kent Brockman mishears a joke and retells it by saying "I guess you can say I'm Iraqi.")
Episode – "Milhouse of Sand and Fog" (Homer sees a newspaper which reads, "TRUCKERS NEEDED IN IRAQ.")
Ireland (Irish: Éire), officially the Republic of Ireland, is an European island in the North Atlantic west of Great Britain.

Allegedly, the Prime Minister of Ireland was a severe alcoholic, as a drunken Homer during a drinking fest at Moe's Tavern during St. Patrick's Day put a beer barrel over his head and proceeded after getting everyone's attention to claim he was the Prime Minister of Ireland, much to the amusement of the tavern patrons.
In their divorce Margerine of Aragon got half of King Henry VIII's kingdom, but she was disappointed when she learned her half was Ireland.

The Simpsons once visited Ireland when Grampa told them he wanted to visit a pub he used to go, the O'Flanagan's Pub. While Homer and Grampa were at the pub, Marge, Bart, and Lisa visited the Blarney Castle, the Giant's Causeway and the Guinness Brewery.
Episode – "Please Homer, Don't Hammer 'Em" ("Smiles of Ireland" book)
Israel (Hebrew: ישראל, Arabic: إسرائيل, Yiddish: ישראל) is a country of Asia.
The Simpsons and the Flanders family visited Israel along with several members of the First Church of Springfield.

Italy (Italian: Italia), officially the Italian Republic (Italian: Repubblica Italiana), is a country in Europe and the capital city is Rome. The country has the world's largest number of World Heritage Sites (55) and a many tourist attractions such as: The Leaning Tower of Pisa, the Colosseum, Pompeii, Lake Como, the Amalfi Coast, and the Venice Canals. Since 1 January 2002, the currency of Italy has been the Euro, prior to this, they used the Lira.

The Simpsons visited Italy when Homer was given the task of picking up Mr. Burns' new Lamborgotti Fasterossa and shipping it back to Springfield. Along the way, they encountered Sideshow Bob and he took up his old vendetta against Bart and the rest of the family.

Several Springfielders are from Italy, including: Sideshow Bob's second wife and son (Francesca Terwilliger and Gino Terwilliger), Giuseppe Granfinali, Enrico Irritazio and Don Vittorio DiMaggio (who once referred to himself as an "old Italian stereotype"). In addition, a number of Springfielders have stereotypical Italian accents, such as Luigi Risotto and Fat Tony. Moe Szyslak used to have an Italian accent, hinting at part-Italian heritage. Also, Milhouse speaks fluent Italian, having learned it from his maternal grandmother, Nana Sophie Mussolini, who is Italian.
In The Simpsons Game, Italy is also a place in the eating contest Around the World in 80 Bites.
Jamaica is an island country in the Caribbean.
Homer tried to prove his coolness so toke Bart and Lisa to Hullabalooza music festival where he bought a Rastafarian hat and then got called a "fat Jamaican guy." ("Homerpalooza")

The Simpsons are following Chuck Garabedian's "Mega-Saver" tip by waiting at the airport for no-show passengers to then buy their seats at a significant discount. The departure board had a flight to Kingston and Homer urges the family to go to Jamaica wearing his Rastafarian hat and giant medallion with "One Love." ("Thirty Minutes Over Tokyo")
Jamaica's flag can be seen in the 2010 Winter Olympics. ("Boy Meets Curl")
Japan was the originator of the Osaka Flu epidemic in Springfield (a Japanese worker who was sick with the flu coughed into several packages that were headed for Springfield).
Homer also made a phone call to Japan after finding out his resemblance to Mr. Sparkle, a dishwasher detergent mascot who originated from Japan.
Nelson Muntz represented Japan in the Model U.N. Club.

The Simpson family once visited Japan, ending up losing all their resources. Most of their money was used up to bail Homer and Bart out of jail after an incident during a sumo wresting match where, due to Homer's stupidity, they ended up throwing Emperor Akihito into a washbin for worn mawashi, and the few cash remaining was lost to the wind when Homer made it into an origami. They were then forced to work at the Osaka Seafood Concern to build up the funds necessary for a plane ticket and later to take part in a game show to win tickets back to Springfield. They win after being embarrassed on the show, which is revealed to be the main idea, and get to go home.

A crazy old man (Grampa) in Springfield, Japan, is constantly mocked because every day he drops a perfect doughnut into the ocean with his grandchildren (Bart and Lisa). When asked why he does such things, he says that is because if he doesn't, a huge sea monster will rise and destroy the city. Instead of understanding, they just laugh at him. One day, while he is preparing a donut for his "ritual", he chokes on the toppings and dies. With nobody to drop the donuts, the sea monster, Homerzilla wakes up and destroys the city.

Bart watches a TV show called 'Battling Seizure Robots' while in Japan. It results in the family having seizures. 'Battling Seizure Robots' is based on an early episode of Pokémon called "Electric Soldier Porygon". This episode was banned worldwide as it caused over 600 Japanese children to have epileptic seizures.
This episode was banned worldwide as it caused over 600 Japanese children to have epileptic seizures.
According to Homer, 'the guy who washes the elephants at the zoo is Japanese, and is called Takashi'. He is in Homer's book club.
The currency of Japan, the Yen's exchange rate is, 1 dollar is worth about 100 yen; with 1 Yen being equivalent to 1 cent.
New neighbors of the Simpsons are originally from Jordan and Bart befriends the son who is the same age Bashir bin Laden.

Kenya is a country in Africa. The capital is Nairobi, and the country achieved independence from the United Kingdom in December 1963. The oldest city and former capital is Mombasa, and 85% of the population is Christian. The currency is the Kenyan Shilling (KES).
Kiribati (pronounced Kiribas) is a country of Oceania .
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Kiribati is one of the countries seen upon the globe.

Kosovo is a country in south-east Europe. Formerly part of Yugoslavia, the country gained independence from Serbia in 2008. Known as the country with the youngest age, Kosovo is one of the most rising countries in the arena. It was on war with Serbia in order to gain its freedom. The war happened in 1999 but there are still consequences that Kosovars face.
Lisa won a radio competition for the family to see the movie Kosovo Autumn.
Laos (Lao: ປະເທດລາວ, officially the Lao People's Democratic Republic (Lao: ສາທາລະນະລັດ ປະຊາທິປະໄຕ ປະຊາຊົນລາວ), is a country in Southeast Asia.
Life Magazine reads, "Bloodbath in Laos." When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Laos is one of the countries seen upon the globe.
Lebanon (Arabic: لبنان, French: Liban) is a country in the Middle East. It is bordered by Syria and Israel and is located on the coast of the Mediterranean Sea.

Lebanon is a developing nation. It once had a flourishing economy, but the Lebanese liquidity crisis, nationwide corruption and recent disasters such as the 2020 Beirut explosion, have precipitated the collapse of Lebanon's currency and fomented political instability, widespread resource shortages, and high unemployment and poverty.

Libya (Arabic: ليبيا) is a country in northern Africa. The capital city and largest city is Tripoli. It is bordered by Tunisia to the northwest, Algeria to the west, Niger to the southwest, Chad to the south, Sudan to the southeast, Egypt to the east, and the Mediterranean Sea to the north.
Bart plays the Libyan U.N. representative in the Model U.N. Club.
During World War II, Libya was controlled by the France (Fezzan) and the United Kingdom which consists of England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland (Cyrenaica and Tripolitania).
Lithuania (officially Republic of Lithuania) is a country in Europe. About 86% people of Lithuania are Lithuanians. The first appearance was in Boy Meets Curl.
Episode – "Hardly Kirk-ing" (Lithuanian centre was seen)
Moe Szyslak's grandmother, Baba Branka, was Macedonian. This makes the Szyslak family trace at least parts of their roots to the region. Moe retained this identity and is still fluent in Macedonian.

Aeropos Walkov, a rising Macedonian baseball star, signs for the Springfield Isotopes after seeing how Springfield is basically the same as Macedonia and he won't feel homesick, and Moe acts as his Macedonian interpreter. His popularity makes Macedonian national costumes a trend in America. Eventually, after a betting scandal, Walkov signs off to another team and Moe returns to his bartending life.
Madagascar is a country of Africa and the 4th largest island in the world.
Malaysia is a country of southeast Asia.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Malaysia is one of the countries seen on the globe.
After Moe has bar transformed into a night, Moe mentions that he no longer sales anymore Duff Beer but instead he sells Malaysian beer that's better than Duff. It's made out of soy sauce.
The Monroe family have a book from Malaysia.

Malta is an island country in Southern Europelocated in the Mediterranean Sea. It consists of an archipelago 80 km (50 mi) south of Italy, 284 km (176 mi) east of Tunisia, and 333 km (207 mi) north of Libya.The two official languages are Maltese and English. The country's capital is Valletta, which is the smallest capital city in the EU by both area and population. With a population of about 542,000 over an area of 316 km2 (122 sq mi),Malta is the world's tenth-smallest country by area and the ninth most densely populated. Various sources consider the country to consist of a single urban region, for which it is often described as a city-state.
Malta is seen during the Model U.N. Club
Mexico is a country in North America. It is located immediately south of the USA.
Patty and Selma show slides to their sister, nephew, and nieces of their trip to Yucatán, Mexico.
Mexico's soccer team played against Portugal's in a match held in Springfield, with disastrous results that ultimately caused a townwide riot.

Sideshow Bob (disguised as Walt Warren) kidnapped Bart and took him to Five Corners, with Walt and the Simpson family in pursuit. Along the way, Bob left a false trail indicating that he was headed for Mexico. Walt saw through the deception and continued to Five Corners, but the Simpsons followed the false lead and went to Mexico, where Homer managed to get into an argument with a cook over the question of "How do you say 'taco' in Mexican?" In Bart to the Future, there is a wall in the border of USA with a Mexico in future.
Wendell Borton represented Mexico in the Model U.N. Club.

When Krusty announces his new hamburger "El atascador", he says, "If you find a hamburger with more fat, you are in Mexico." In the Latin American version, it was replaced with "I'll give you a prize."
Krusty's vanity passion project movie The Sands of Space was shot in Mexico in the Chihuahuan Desert around Juárez.
Mexico was also one of the countries featured in Around the World in 80 Bites, which is a level of The Simpsons Game.
In the segment, The Devil and Homer Simpson it was mentioned that Devil Flanders finds it easy to take souls in Mexico than United States.
Homer mistakes Morocco with Monaco. He asks why Princess Grace would live in a place like this. Seen represented at the 2010 Winter Olympics.
When devising what to do with Burns' taxed money, Lisa imagines she is being spent of virtual reality machines. She pictures 1200 AD Mongolia and Ghengis Khan greets her.

Morocco is a country in the continent of Africa, located in the northwest part of the continent. The Capital City is Rabat. Morocco is bordered by Algeria to the east, Western Sahara to the south, and the Mediterranean Sea to the north.

After Mr. Burns was forcibly removed from power by Homer Simpson, he and Smithers went to Marrakesh, Morocco. Mr. Burns considered giving back to society, but he intended to have a lot of Opium first, asking Smithers to get some, although Smithers was reluctant to do so. Smithers then asks a vendor where to find drugs, who then reveals that most of his products were made out of drugs, including the Monkey, although he needs American money to pay for it as their own currency "was made of drugs." Smithers eventually was arrested for a drug bust and sentenced to do time at a Turkish prison for 80 years, although he ended up being oddly happy to be taken there.

The Simpsons go to Morocco when they get the Monkey Paw. Homer also ended up confusing Morocco with Monaco (the country where the late Princess Grace lived in). When they attempted to leave the country, Homer ended up getting busted by flight security for attempting to smuggle souvenirs out of the country and ended up having to pay a two American dollar fine as his punishment, despite being held up by security.

Morocco only appeared twice; it first appeared in Treehouse of Horror II, and made a reappearance in C.E. D'oh. Due to all Treehouse of Horror episodes being non-canon, the Simpsons most probably never actually went to the country. Mr. Burns, however, did visit the country after being ousted by Homer Simpson.
Homer says to Marge: "Face it, Marge. Catholics Rule. We got Boston, South America, the *good* part of Ireland and we're making serious inroads in Mozambique, baby!"
Myanmar, (Burmese: မြန်မာ), (formerly Burma) officially the Republic of the Union of Myanmar (Burmese: ပြည်ထောင်စု သမ္မတ မြန်မာနိုင်ငံတော်), is a country located in Asia.
When Krusty was told that he had won the Nobel Peace Prize, he was taken to The Hague in the Netherlands instead of Oslo, Norway; to be tried in the International Court of Justice.
New Zealand (Māori: Aotearoa) is a country in Oceania 386 miles (621 km) from Australia.
Kurt Hardwick and Ethan Ballantyne, the counselors of the Arts Camp, are from Wellington. They speak with New Zealand accents and have their country's flag hanging in their apartment in Sprooklyn.
Tag Tuckerbag, host of The Amazing Place', has a thick New Zealand accent and voiced by New Zealander Rhys Darby.

Taika Waititi (voiced by the real Taika Waititi) is onboard the Pacific Princess Leia cruise around Lake Springfield as part of secret scheme to steal Comic Book Guy's rare Radioactive Man action figure. When Taika Waititi is caught, he cites how he has powerful friends in New Zealand and listing off names of famous New Zealanders.

Marge injures her back and gets physical therapy and kitesurfing lessons from Nigel who just moved to Springfield from his native New Zealand except Nigel is just a cover for Russian spy Dimitri Nigelovonovitch. His mission fails in large part because Homer can't understand his phony New Zealand accent.
New Zealander director Peter Jackson (voiced by the real Peter Jackson) introduces even more unused footage from his own documentary The Beatles: Get Back.

Students at Springfield Elementary are using ChatHippo, an ad supported free chat website, for online classes during lockdown. Principal Skinner has to read out ads including one for OddsMonster.Crypto which Bart checks out for team stats on New Zealand Rules Rugby. The clock shows Bart has been on the site from 8:33 to 2:54 when says to himself in exasperation, "Whatever rugby is Wellington sucks at it."

In "Bart 'N' Frink", the Simpsons and Professor Frink travel there for a college reunion at the mansion of his classmate Peter Linz. Due to an NDA, the country's name (and Lisa's souvenir) are censored; however, Frink did not sign the NDA and he says "New Zealand" uncensored. As their plane takes off, the country becomes visible from above.

New Zealander Lucy Lawless (voiced by the real Lucy Lawless) is kidnapped by the Collector (Comic Book Guy's supervillain alter ego) . She constantly has to correct others after they call her Xena that she is Lucy Lawless and not her TV character Xena the Warrior Princess.

In Simpsons Comics #68: "Merchants of Vengeance!" Homer, after a bender, ends up as Prime Minister of New Zealand. Later a newspaper in Springfield has the headline "Australia Declares War on New Zealand" on the front page.
Molloy, the Springfield Cat Burglar, is not specified as being from anywhere specific, but his voice was New Zealander Sam Neill.

During Larry’s funeral, Marge asked why Homer Simpson, Moe Szyslak, Carl Carlson and Lenny Leonard never wanted to get to know him. To which Moe responded that at least they came and that Barney Gumble never bothered to, while in Nicaragua, Barney is helping a small village to get water.

Nigeria (Hausa: Najeriya, Hausa (Ajami script): نجري, Igbo: Naịjirịa, Yoruba: Nàìjíríà) a West African country. Its capital city is Abuja, and it has been ruled over by several tribal chiefdoms and kingdoms over the millennia. It has the 20th largest economy in the world and has a diverse geography. Nigeria is also culturally and ethnically diverse, with its largest ethnic groups being the Yoruba, Ibo and Hausa.
Niue is an island in the Pacific Ocean. It is owned by New Zealand.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Niue is one of the islands seen upon the globe.
North Korea (Korean: 북한), officially the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (Korean: 조선민주주의인민공화국), is a country in East Asia. Its current director is Kim Jong-un, the son of the late Kim Jong-il.

During Sideshow Bob's flashback, he was seen trying to determine which country he should start his life over in. He decided to grab a globe, spin it, and stab a knife on it. Which ever country the knife landed on, Bob would go and live there. One of the countries the knife landed on is North Korea, to which Bob exclaimed "Damn it!" and spun the globe again, not wanting to live there.

While working at the Department of Motor Vehicles, Wayne Slater angrily recounts to Sideshow Mel the time he was captured and held in North Korea. He was forced to write a musical play about Kim Jong-il, the country's leader.

Bart is showing off his new Slipreme Caliphates 2 on the school playground, but the shoes immediately fall apart because they are made from used Band-Aids and North Korean newspapers. Nelson reads one of the clippings aloud, "Dear Leader Announces Wheat Quotas Exceeded Yet Again" and all the children start cheering.
Norway is a country in Europe. Its capital is Oslo.
The Simpsons' creator Matt Groening's mother Margaret Wiggum-Groening's family originated from Norway, from Oppland County, the original surname being Veggum.

In "Coming to Homerica", Marge said that Maggie's first word was in Ogdenville dialect (Norwegian), but Maggie had spoken earlier. Marge had not heard this. In the same episode, it is revealed that the people from Ogdenville are mostly immigrants from Norway.
Norway is one of the countries most often referred to in the Simpsons episodes.
A Norwegian foreign exchange student came over to North Haverbrook and impregnated Darcy. By the time she realized it, he had already returned to Norway, or Norwegia, as Darcy said.

Pakistan (Urdu: پاکستان), officially the Islamic Republic of Pakistan (Urdu: اسلامی جمہوریہ پاكستان), is a country in South Asia. It is the fifth-most populous nation in the world, and is bordered by Iran, Afghanistan, India, and China. The country was historically the Punjab territory of British India, until India and Pakistan were granted independence from the United Kingdom on 15 August 1947. Pakistan became a republic in 1956. The capital city is Islamabad, but the largest city is Karachi. The currency is the Pakistani Rupee (PKR).
Krusty the Clown remarked that India nuked Pakistan.
Thirty years into the future it is shown that the United States has debt to pay to Pakistan along with several other countries.

Panamanian dictator General Noriega was ousted by U.S. in Panama in 1989 and former First Lady Barbara Bush mentions "Too bad got off on the wrong foot. It's just like the Noriega thing. Now, he and George are the best of friends." and . Bart plays Panamanian Strongman at the Noise Land Video Arcade where the aviator is a hulked out Noriega at the top of a building being shot at by airplanes in a parody King Kong climbing the Empire State Building. Within the game the building's flagpole has the flag of Panama.

The Sea Captain's container ship carrying America's entire supply of cigarettes is jammed up in the Panama Canal. Patty and Selma see the news report from the Panama Canal and start hording after the warning of a cigarette shortage.
Episode – "Boy-Scoutz 'n the Hood" (Panamanian Strongman)
Episode – "Bart Stops to Smell the Roosevelts" (Panama Canal)
Episode – "Women in Shorts" (Panama Canal)

The first episode of The Simpsons aired on December 17, 1989, just three days before Operation Just Cause or the U.S. Invasion of Panama was ordered by then President George H.W. Bush that toppled down General Manuel Noriega.
Papua New Guinea is a country in Oceania, near Australia.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Papua New Guinea is one of the countries seen upon the globe.

Amber Dempsey is said to have traveled to Paraguay to get eyelash implants, as they were apparently banned in the United States. When Homer goes over the list of countries that have better health insurance than the United States, he tells Marge that, while Canada, Japan, Sweden, Great Britain, and all of Europe have better insurance, "...you can thank your lucky stars we don't live in Paraguay," which causes Marge to cry.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Paraguay is one of the countries seen upon the globe.

The Simpson family (except for Bart) visited Peru when Bart discovered a tracking chip in his cell phone and put it on a bird which turned out to be going to Machu Picchu in Peru. Marge had previously put the chip in Bart's phone to track his movements, so believing that the chip was still with Bart, Marge and Homer took Lisa and Maggie to Peru in search of Bart, while they were really following the bird. Whilst all of this was happening, Bart was at home on his own and was starting to get scared. After the Simpson return home Lisa discovers that Maggie is missing and questions Homer to where she is. It is than shown that they lift Maggie back at Machu Picchu.

The Philippines (Filipino: Pilipinas, Spanish: Filipinas), officially the Republic of the Philippines (Filipino: Republika ng Pilipinas, Spanish: República de las Filipinas), is a country of Southeast Asia. The current president is Ferdinand Marcos.
When Lisa unwraps Bart's globe, Philippines is one of the countries seen upon the globe.
Krusty the Clown also decided to repackage the Krusty-Brand Home Pregnancy Test as coffee stirrers and sell them in the Philippines after the test was found to give "20% false 'Hey-Hey's."

One of Homer's videos is "Philippine prisonered," which is a reference to the Dancing Inmates of the Cebu Provincial Detention and Rehabilitation Center in the Philippines. The Dancing Inmates became YouTube sensations thanks to their interpretations of several Michael Jackson songs, including "Thriller" and "They Don't Care About Us."
The earliest seasons of The Simpsons aired in Cartoon Network Philippines from April 2006 to 2007.

Poland (Polish: Polska), officially the Republic of Poland (Polish: Rzeczpospolita Polska), is a country in Europe, the modern-day Republic of Poland came into existence in 1989 after forty-two years of Communist rule. The country joined the European Union on 1 May 2004 and is the sixth-most populated country in the EU, with 38,500,000 inhabitants. The current Prime Minister is Donald Tusk, who has served since 13 December 2023.
Poland was invaded by Nazi Germany in September 1939. The invasion triggered World War II after France and the United Kingdom declared war of the Third Reich.
Milhouse Van Houten represents Poland at the Model U.N. Club.
Bart insults the Polish International Olympic Committee by saying "Poland, ey? Haha. Too easy."
Poland was mentioned in an advertisement for "The Clogger" in the Polish dub of The Simpsons Movie.
Portugal is a country in Europe, Located on the Iberian Peninsula which shares a border with Spain.

The Simpsons watch a TV commercial: "The Continental Soccer Association is coming to Springfield! This match will determine once and for all which nation is the greatest on Earth – Mexico or Portugal!" and convinces them and the rest of Springfield to go, but a riot breaks out from the American crowd's boredom at watching soccer.
Kent Brockman remarks, "Hang onto each new development like so many Romanian trapeze artists."
During the 2010 Winter Olympics the Romanian flag can be seen.

(Discussing foreign Krusty, to make more money, says the foreign rights to his show, but upset when he becomes the least popular Krusty. He asked, "What about Romanian Krusty?" and his agent replies, "You mean President for life Krusty?"

Lugash's origins are left somewhat opaque except for him defecting into East Germany. The stage direction in script online notes he speaks with a very thick Romanian accent. Romania had a long history success in women's gymnastics at international competitions.

Russia, also known as the Russian Federation, is a country in Eurasia and is currently the largest country in the world. It was formerly part of the Soviet Union, or the U.S.S.R (Union of Soviet Socialist Republics), a former federation of communist republics that occupied the northern half of Asia and part of eastern Europe from the Russian Revolution of 1917 with the Romanov family executed until the collapse and then formal dissolution on December 26, 1991.

In "Simpson Tide", when the Captain goes to check on a problem in the torpedo hatch, Homer is left in charge. At this point, an enemy submarine approaches Homer's, and Homer immediately responds by giving the order to fire a torpedo. However, Captain Tenille is in the torpedo hatch and is fired at the other submarine. The other sub fires back at them, and Homer gives the order to retreat. The submarine ends up in Russian waters and is interpreted by the United States government as an attempt to defect. This event creates a political schism between the USA and Russia; the Russian government reveals that the Soviet Union still exists, and that its fall was merely a ruse. Nuclear war is anticipated until the US Navy drops depth charges on Homer's sub, aiming either to destroy it or force it to surface. The consequent explosion causes a pinhole leak in the submarine's wall. The crew all believe their situation is fatal until Homer remembers the earring he took from Bart. He uses it to plug the leak and saves the submarine.It was secretly preparing to openly turn back into the Soviet Union (which faked its fall) when no one knew about it. Homer got into trouble with them while he was in the navy.

Jakaya Kiwete, who succeeded the real-life President of Tanzania who was serving when the episode aired, greatly invested in expanding Tanzania's tourism industry when he served as President between 2005 and 2015. https://allafrica.com/stories/201504080685.html Kitenge, The Simpson family's tour guide in the country, would become President after overthrowing former President Muntu before the family departed. Kitenge is also the name for a clothing fabric worn by many Muslim women in African countries.https://www.pinterest.com/rizq0748/kitenge-muslim-wear/ https://www.pinterest.com/pin/855121047972474892/

Many of the references to Vietnam in The Simpsons are about the Vietnam War which was between South Vietnam and the communist North Vietnam and its allied guerilla forces in South Vietnam known as the Viet Cong. The involvement of the United States in the Vietnam War on the side of South Vietnam began in the 1950s and greatly escalated in 1965 until its withdrawal in 1973.

Principal Seymour Skinner is a veteran of this war which was first mentioned in the third season episode "Separate Vocations" when upon seeing the Springfield Elementary School's puma mascot statue egged and toilet papered laments to himself, "I saw some awful things in 'Nam but you really have to wonder at the mentality that would desecrate a helpless puma." This quick mention was just meant by the writers as a throwaway joke, but his experience in and immediately after Vietnam became a major part of Principal Skinner's character.

Other Vietnam veterans include Goose Gladwell ("Whoa, I must warn you, sir. I was a Green Beret in Vietnam. The things I saw there are what made me crazy!"), the Saloon Bartender at Ye Olde Animatronic Saloon ("I got this metal ass in 'Nam defending this country for lazy jerks like you.") and Clyde, whose sob story single-handily stops Marge's plan to finally put out the Springfield tire fire.

Zimbabwe is a landlocked country in Africa, which borders South Africa, Mozambique, Botswana and Zambia. The country has a population of approximately 16,000,000 people and has sixteen official languages; with English, Shona and Ndebele the most commonly used. Prior to officially recognised independence from the United Kingdom in 1980, the country was known as "Rhodesia" but upon the landslide electoral victory of Robert Mugabe in 1980, the country was renamed "Zimbabwe". Mugabe would remain in office as Prime Minister from 1980-87, until he became President of Zimbabwe and abolished the position of Prime Minister. He remained in office for another thirty years, until being held under house arrest in November 2017 during a coup d'etat; where he anounced his resignation aged 93 on 21 November before impeachment proceedings could take place. The current and 3rd President of Zimbabwe is Emmerson Mnangagwa, who was inaugurated in Harare on 24 November 2017, and was sacked from the vice-presidency of Zimbabwe by Mugabe one week prior to the coup. It was widely speculated that Mugabe had preferred his wife Grace to succeed him as president. The coup followed years of economic mismanagment, corruption and power abuses.

Zimbabwe has experienced hyperinflation since February 2007. During the height of inflation from 2008 to 2009, it was difficult to measure Zimbabwe's hyperinflation because the government of Zimbabwe stopped filing official inflation statistics. In April 2009, Zimbabwe stopped printing its currency, and currencies from other countries were used. In mid-2015, Zimbabwe announced plans to have completely switched to the US dollar by the end of that year.

Grampa visited here when he served in the army during World War II and met Edwina there, who he ended up having a daughter with. The capital city is London, where the Simpsons visit (despite a teenage, high-school Homer once claiming that he would never go to England). The London Eye is one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world along with Westminster Abbey, the Tower of London and Buckingham Palace. Nigel Bakerbutcher revealed to the citizens of Springfield that the whole of London is under constant surveillance with CCTV cameras everywhere. While visiting London, Homer's rental car crashes through the gates of Buckingham Palace and into Queen Elizabeth II's carriage. He is imprisoned in the Tower of London, but manages to escape the tower and ends up in Buckingham Palace; where he asks the Queen to find it in her "jewel-encrusted" heart to forgive him. She forgives him and lets him return home to America as long as he takes Madonna back with him.

After the train station terrorism incident, Mayor Quimby that everything he said about terrorism was to cause fear, but now he says it's all true. To protect the city from all future attacks, they hired an external consultant from London, England. After Homer and Ned disable all the cameras in Springfield, it is revealed that the antics of Springfield were broadcast as a reality show in Britain, known as The American Oafs, with Queen Elizabeth saying she'll miss Ralph Wiggum, as he reminds her of her own son, Prince Charles, who walks in and tells his mother that his cat's breath smells like cat food.
Furthermore, it is possible that the country which Abraham Simpson claimed to have come from was England.

State of Springfield Dancin' Homer - The family moves to Capital City when Homer is promoted by Antoine Tex O'Hara to the team in Capital City after the Isotopes keep winning more games thanks to Homer as the Springfield Isotopes mascot, "Dancin' Homer". Bart vs. Lisa vs. the Third Grade - After Bart and Lisa are in third grade at the same time, they are made buddies as part of the buddy system on a field trip to Capital City, and are forced to work together after they get lost there. Lemon of Troy - Bart, Milhouse, Nelson, Martin, Todd and Database wage war on Shelbyville after their beloved town lemon tree comes up missing. Meanwhile, Homer, Ned, Kirk, Martin Prince, Sr. and Database's father take Ned's RV to search for their sons and bring back their lemon tree. The Severn-Beer Snitch - The family visits Shelbyville, but the citizens of Shelbyville call Springfield's residents "hicks". Marge vs. the Monorail - Marge immediately drives to North Haverbrook, where Lyle Lanley mentioned that it was a previous purchaser of one of his monorails. Lyle Lanley sold monorails to Ogdenville and Brockway. You Only Move Twice - Homer moves the family to Cypress Creek after he receives a better job offer at a better nuclear power plant, but Homer is oblivious to the fact that his new boss is Hank Scorpio, a James Bond-style super villain who is out to rule the world. Midnight Towboy - Homer works as a tow truck operator in Guidopolis. Later, after Homer gets abducted by Louie and other tow truck drivers in a trap hole in his basement in Guidopolis, Maggie saves Homer there by using her independent abilities and riding on Santa's Little Helper. Summer of 4 Ft. 2 - Ned makes a deal with Homer to watch over his beach house in Little Pwagmattasquarmsettport while he serves jury duty. The Simpson family head out to the beach house, with Marge suggesting that Bart could bring Milhouse and Lisa bring a friend too (only that Lisa has no friends). Little Big Girl - When Bart gets annoyed by Homer's errands after he gets awarded with a driver's license, he flees to North Haverbrook, where he has a relationship with an older girl named Darcy. Cape Feare - The Simpson family enter the Witness Relocation Program, nicknamed as "The Thompsons" and move to Terror Lake, a "Cape Fear"-esque town several miles away in order to avoid Sideshow Bob. Waverly Hills 9-0-2-1-D'oh - When Marge discovers the poor teaching conditions at Springfield Elementary, she gets Homer to rent an apartment in the Waverly Hills area so Bart and Lisa can attend school there.

Dancin' Homer - The family moves to Capital City when Homer is promoted by Antoine Tex O'Hara to the team in Capital City after the Isotopes keep winning more games thanks to Homer as the Springfield Isotopes mascot, "Dancin' Homer".

When giving testimony in court, Homer claimed that his memory was "hazy". The Blue-Haired Lawyer responded with the question "Hazy like the moors of Scotland?", pointing to Willie who'd been wrongly arrested for stealing Homer's car.

When Bart and Lisa become best friends after Milhouse leaves for Capital City with his mother, Willie claims it will not last as brother and sister are natural enemies. Like Englishmen and Scots, or Welshmen and Scots, or Japanese and Scots, or Scots and other Scots. Damn Scots! They ruined Scotland!

In the level Around the World in 80 Bites Willie climbs to the roof of a big wall and closes the gate to Scotland, yelling "Screw you!". Bart gets to the top of the "Eiffel Tower" and glides to the wall where he opens the gate.

The Old Country is the land which Abraham Simpson claimed to have immigrated from in "Much Apu About Nothing." This country, most likely Scotland or Northern England, is said to be where the lineage of the Simpson family originated.

Abe says he is unable to remember exactly which country he is from, likely due to his poor memory and his dementia. However, it is (at least, according to his own memory) an English-speaking nation, probably European and possibly Celtic, and is likely a country in which the Simpson surname is well-established. An important cautionary note is that any of the places implied by Abe could be the often inaccurate and sometimes contradictory imaginings of his aged memory. The Old Country could be any one of the following nations:

Canada is another possibility as this is where, if Abe's story is to be trusted, Virgil and Mabel Simpson fled to in 1860 and had a son, Abraham Simpson I (Abe's great-grandfather) in 1861. Also, Homer has been hinted at having Native American ancestry, a heritage which is very uncommon outside of North America. Words from Abe in "Midnight Rx" could either support or oppose the Canada theory, depending on how they are interpreted. Canadians whom reside in the Springfield area include State Comptroller Atkins and Tyler.

In "The Color Yellow," Abe mentions, perhaps senilely, that his family were kicked out of Australia. The Simpson name has been found in Australia and New Zealand since the 1800s, having being brought there by convicts from the British Isles.

The accents spoken by Abe's father, Orville, could also be described as similar to those of the North of England. The pronunciation of 'tomorrow' discussed in the Scotland section above is often heard in Tyneside and Sunderland accents as well, as are the long "a" and "o" sounds. The way Orville pronounces 'going' and 'home' is somewhat similar to the way that people from the north of England (Newcastle, Yorkshire, Manchester, Lancaster, Cheshire, Cumberland etc.) pronounce these words. The style of buildings and the clothing of background characters are similar to rural Yorkshire. Furthermore, the first record of the surname 'Simpson' was in England, during the 1350s and the surname is common throughout the country, particularly in the North West. As with the Scottish, people from Northern England, particularly Yorkshire are often stereotyped as being tight with money, as shown by Abe in "Bart's New Friend." If The Simpsons Uncensored Family Album is canon, the surnames of women marrying into the Simpsons being overwhelmingly English in origin may favor the England theory, for instance, Hickman, Abe's mother's maiden-name, Dinsdale, a northern surname, Crouse, Stillman and Ethridge.

Tibet is a large region in Asia. Officially, is part of (and is owned by) China. Many people, including Lisa, want to free Tibet. There is a world campaign called the 'Free Tibet' campaign.

When Lisa wins the spelling bee, she shouts “Free Tibet!” The Dalai Lama, the Buddhist leader of Tibet, makes an appearance, in which Mr. Burns forces Homer (disguised as Pie Man) to throw a pie in the Dalai Lama's face.

In 'Goo Goo Gai Pan', when the Simpsons visit China, Madam Wu asks Lisa if she is happy to have an adopted sister. Lisa replies, “Well, I dunno.....I'm considered pretty...smart.” Wu replies, “Well, Tibet was considered pretty independent, but now we know the answer to that.” Wu laughs, but Lisa gets angry.

The Simpsons has been censored or faced controversy on Turkish television: The episode "Mobile Homer" is unavailable on the Turkish version of Disney+, due to the unflatting misrepresentation of Turks, the country's ethnic people. CNBC-e, the show's broadcaster, was fined 52,951 lira (around $1,461.30) by the Radio and Television Supreme Council. The fine had been levied due to a scene in "Treehouse of Horror XXII" where the Devil orders God a coffee, thus CNBC-e was "making fun of God".

The country and its culture have been featured in and parodied many times on The Simpsons over the years (alongside France, it is the most frequently depicted foreign country).
